1998-05-01.title: Before Celebrating a Nation, Remember the 20,000 Soldiers Who Died Defending It
1998-05-01.abstract: Israel's Indpendence Day, year's most joyous day, follows directly on Memorial Day, its most painful; since a day in Jewish calendar starts previous evening and ends at sundown, Israel's 50th birthday party--fireworks, street music, dancing--takes off at sundown after 24 hours of wrenching commemoration for the more than 20,000 soldiers who have fallen in battle (Reporter's Notebook column) (M)

1998-05-01.title: Clinton Is 'Encouraged' by Iraqi Progress on Arms Inspections
1998-05-01.abstract: Pres Clinton says that he is encouraged by Iraqi cooperation with United Nations weapons inspectors and that he expects to reduce number of American troops who are now deployed in Persian Gulf region to keep watch on Iraq (M)

1998-05-01.title: U.S. SET TO BACK $1 BILLION OUTLAY TO AID INDONESIA
1998-05-01.abstract: United States is preparing to back $1 billion payment in international emergency aid to Indonesia, despite evidence that Pres Suharto's family and friends are continuing to undermine efforts to break up multibillion-dollar monopolies they control; Treasury officials acknowledge that Indonesia has failed to meet some of most important conditions that were imposed by International Monetary Fund in return for $40 billion bailout package; say, nevertheless, that United States expects to vote in favor of gradual resumption of aid when monetary fund's board meets next week; Clinton Adm says it is satisfied that Indonesia is making 'adequate progress' toward meeting IMF demands (M)

1998-05-01.title: U.S. Report Calls Iran No. 1 Terror Sponsor, Despite New Leader
1998-05-01.abstract: United States State Dept, in its annual report on international terrorism, contends that Iran remains 'most active state sponsor of terrorism' despite last year's election of a more moderate President, Mohammad Khatami, and 'more conciliatory' rhetoric adopted by Khatami and his deputies; report, required by Congress, finds that Teheran Govt was responsible for at least 13 assassinations last year, most of them in northern Iraq against members of Iran's main opposition groups; number of casualties from international terrorism dropped last year: 221 people died in terrorist acts in 1997, compared with 314 in 1996; more than third of 304 acts of international terrorism took place in Colombia; deadliest terrorist attack took place in Egypt, when 58 foreign tourists and 4 Egyptians were killed in attack by Muslim extremists at temple near Luxor; State Dept singles out seven state sponsors of terorism: Cuba, Iran, Iraq, Libya, North Korea, Sudan and Syria (M)

1998-05-01.title: Yeltsin's New Outsider Team: Young, Reformist and Green
1998-05-01.abstract: Russian Pres Boris N Yeltsin assembles new team that is strikingly young, pro-reform and from outside Moscow; Russian political establishment was stunned when Yeltsin selected 35-year-old Sergei V Kiriyenko as his new Prime Minnister; Yeltsin went further by filling out top ranks of his Govt with squad of young politicians cut from same cloth; key appointment is Yeltsin tapping Oleg Sysuyev as third of three deputies to Kiriyenko; appointment is a major setback to Russia's business tycoons, as well as Communists, who had hoped to install one of ther own at pinnacle orf new Govt (M)

1998-05-02.title: By Any Measure, Europe's New Monetary Union Is a Historic Exploit
1998-05-02.abstract: WASHINGTON—  Starting from the Declaration of Independence, it took the United States nearly 90 years to establish a fully fledged common currency, and a further 50 years to set up the Federal Reserve system in 1913.

1998-05-02.title: Big Sludge Spill Poisons Land in Southern Spain
1998-05-02.abstract: Break in reservoir of zinc mine in southern Spain releases about 150 million cubic feet of acidic sludge into Guadimar River near Aznalcollar, contaminating croplands and Donana wildlife reserve and causing serious environmental disasters; photo; map; makeshift dikes stop toxic waste from swamping reserve, but poisonous flow has cut through northern part of vital buffer zone around park; heavy-metal pollution will immediately enter food chain during crucial breeding season for many species; Donana Park is on main route for Europe's migratory birds and home to many mammals but farms and tourism have been encroaching on fertile region; environmentalists have warned of danger from Los Frailes Mine thirty miles away; large open-pit mine is owned by Toronto-based Boliden Ltd (M)

1998-05-02.title: War Crimes Horrors Revive As Croat Faces Possible Trial
1998-05-02.abstract: Horrors of war crimes by Nazi-backed Ustashe regime in Croatia are revived with arrest in Argentina of Dinko Sakic, one of most important accused war criminals still alive; Sime Klaic, who was imprisoned in Jasenovac concentration camp run by Sakic, recalls torture and murder of three captured American aviators; Sakic, living in Argentina, discussed his past in broadcast interview last month; Croatia bows to heavy international pressure and requests extradition; trial would compel Croatia to examine its wartime history, but could also serve revisionists like Pres Franjo Tudjman who prefer to minimize Ustashe atrocities; Jewish survivor describes Sakic overseeing execution of several hundred Jewish women and children; other accounts also quoted; brutality of Ustashe regime, which killed 31,000 of Croatia's 39,000 Jews and about 200,000 Serbs, is rarely mentioned in schoolbooks; Sakic's wife's brother headed Ustache secret police, and she is also accused by survivors of committing murders; photos; map (M)

1998-05-02.title: Baptist Gets Visa to Return to Russia
1998-05-02.abstract: Dan Polland, American Baptist missionary expelled from Russia in March, is granted visa to return to his church in Vanino, remote port town in Russian Far East (S)

1998-05-02.title: Clinton Predicts a Safer and Richer Europe
1998-05-02.abstract: Pres Clinton hails Senate approval of NATO membership for Poland, Hungary and Czech Republic, saying it will make Europe safer for its citizens and a more prosperous trading partner for US; comments to workers at Therma Inc in Palo Alto, supplier to high-tech industry; Clinton is in California to visit daughter at Stamford, and to raise money for Democratic candidates in Silicon Valley and Los Angeles (S)

1998-05-03.title: EUROPEANS ACCEPT A SINGLE CURRENCY DESPITE LATE SNAG
1998-05-03.abstract: European Union plan to begin common currency among 11 countries in 1999 gets underway after leaders of 15 member nations, meeting, Brussels, resolve dispute between France and Germany over who should run new European central bank; leaders name Wim F Duisenberg of Netherlands, German-backed candidate, to eight-year term after he says he plans to retire by mid-2002; France had sought written agreement that he would leave after four years to make way for French candidate, Jean-Claude Trichet; photo (L)

1998-05-03.title: Albright Finds Unalloyed Love in a Newly Minted Democracy
1998-05-03.abstract: State Sec Madeleine K Albright visits Mongolia, basking in attention of nation's plucky young democratic reformers; meets with Prime Min Tsahiagiyn Elbegdorj, who says Monglia implements both political and economic changes, while cherishing human rights; photo (M)

1998-05-03.title: Opium Puts Myanmar In Crisis Over AIDS
1998-05-03.abstract: Heroin addiction is spreading in Myanmar, world's top opium exporter, where sharing of syringes has led to world's highest rate of infection with AIDS virus; foreign medical researchers put total number of addicts at some 500,000 and estimate several hundred thousand have become HIV-positive (M)

1998-05-03.title: The Welfare State Is Alive, if Besieged, in Uruguay
1998-05-03.abstract: Economic change sweeping Latin America has largely bypassed Uruguay, where welfare state faces biggest test in years, union-sponsored plebiscite opposing new law to let private companies compete with Government's electric monopoly; unions seek to make Pres Julio Maria Sanguinetti, centrist who pushed law through Congress, into lame duck and propel socialist to presidency next year; map; photo (M)

1998-05-03.title: Clinton Seeks To Overturn Land Mine Moratorium
1998-05-03.abstract: Pres Clinton scrambles to repeal law he signed in Feb 1996 to impose one-year moratorium on use of antipersonnel land mines starting in Feb 1999; Defense Department opposes law, warning of serious threats to American troops around world, although it has no current plans to use mines; Clinton stand angers highly organized and vocal critics of land mines; national security adviser Samuel R Berger has appealed to law's sponsor, Sen Patrick J Leahy, to push for its reversal (M)

1998-05-04.title: Gore, in Mideast, Prods Sides Before Talks
1998-05-04.abstract: Vice Pres Al Gore meets with Prime Min Benjamin Netanyahu in Tel Aviv and Yasir Arafat in West Bank city of Ramallah to press for breakthrough at impeding Israeli-Palestinian peace talks; meets with Pres Hosni Mubarak and other Egyptian officials in Cairo (M)

1998-05-04.title: U.S. Pessimistic Before Middle East Talks
1998-05-04.abstract: United States aides are pessimistic that meetings in London between State Sec Madeleine K Albright and Israeli and Palestinian leaders will lead to breakthrough; suggest Prime Min Benjamin Netanyahu will not accept US call for roughly 13 percent withdrawal from West Bank (M)

1998-05-04.title: Central Bank Debacle May Shake Market Confidence, for Now
1998-05-04.abstract: ROME—  — This weekend's unseemly battle over the presidency of the European Central Bank could rattle currency and bond markets temporarily and might even cause interest rates to rise sooner than expected in Germany and France this spring, several leading European economists said Sunday.

1998-05-04.title: Chirac, the 'Bulldozer,' at the Birth of the Euro
1998-05-04.abstract: Analysis of Pres Jacques Chirac's successful drive to deny full eight-year term to Wim F Duisenberg as head of new European central bank; he has forced Duisenberg, backed by all other European Union leaders and all 15 EU central bankers, to agree to retire early; economists hold move will weaken common currency; photo (M)

1998-05-04.title: How Much Is a Euro? About$1.1
1998-05-04.abstract: LONDON—  Another struggle, another eleventh-hour compromise, and finally, after years of arduous effort, Europe has agreed to launch the euro. So what's it worth?

1998-05-05.title: Gojko Susak, Defense Minister of Croatia, Is Dead at 53
1998-05-05.abstract: Gojko Susak, Croatia's Defense Minister, dies at age 53 from lung cancer; photo (M)

1998-05-05.title: Italy's Leader, Due on Visit, Praises U.S. for Backing Euro
1998-05-05.abstract: Interview with Italian Prime Min Romano Prodi, who will arrive in US for state visit on May 5, and who praises US for supporting euro, new European currency; photo (M)

1998-05-05.title: China's Leading University Celebrates and Ponders
1998-05-05.abstract: Excitement over anticancer drugs angiostatin and endostatin shifts from laboratory to public after reports that tests eradicated tumors in mice with no side effects; many cancer institutes, doctors and cancer support groups report surge of phone calls from those desperate or merely curious; drug manufacturer, Entremed Inc, says it will be 12 to 18 months before first stages of trials on humans begin, and maybe several years before drugs are available on market, if at all; cancer experts say trials could be especially complicated, given that angiostatin and endostatin work unlike other treatments by attacking blood vessels that feed tumors, starving them of blood they need to grow; some warn against putting too much faith in treatment that has not been proved on anything but mice, noting that medicines that often work on mice do not help humans; comments by prominent New York City oncologist Dr Larry Norton noted; Entremed stock soars in trading on May 4, rising by $39.75 per share (M)

1998-05-05.title: They Fear Fewer Jobs, Lower Wages and Pressure for More Productivity : European Unions Warily Greet the Euro
1998-05-05.abstract: FRANKFURT—  It can come as a surprise to hear labor union spokesmen across the Continent expressing only guarded enthusiasm for the single European currency and the streamlined new Europe it is expected to bring.

1998-05-05.title: India's New Defense Chief Sees Chinese Military Threat
1998-05-05.abstract: Indian Defense Min George Fernandes breaches longstanding taboo by declaring publicly that China, not Pakistan, is India's main military threat; says India should move to declare itself nuclear weapons state if review of military policy by India's new Government supports his view; says India should awaken to fact that Chinese military activities and alliances have begun to encircle it (M)

1998-05-06.title: As Gaullist Party Crumbles, Chirac Tries to Quell Factionalism
1998-05-06.abstract: French Pres Jacques Chirac, whose conservative Gaullist party has crumbled around him under weight of financing scandals, election losses and personal rivalries, privately warns supporters to stop bickering or face loss of one of last bastions, namely, Paris City Hall (M)

1998-05-06.title: U.N. Chief Pays a Visit To Tribunal For Rwanda
1998-05-06.abstract: UN Sec Gen Kofi Annan visits International Criminal Tribunal for Rwanda during trial of Col Anatole Nsengiumva, who is charged with inciting Hutu mobs to kill Tutsi during 1994 civil war; says there has been extensive improvements in tribunal over past six months; his suspension of its top administrative officers little over year ago for mismanagement and questionable financial transactions recalled (M)

1998-05-06.title: They Say Life Is Hard Guarding The Pope
1998-05-06.abstract: Family members and acquaintances of Cpl Cedric Tornay, member of Vatican's Swiss Guards, say nothing in his record or behavior in Vatican suggested he would fatally shoot commanding officer, Lieut Col Alois Estermann, and wife Galdys Meza Romano, before committing suicide; as Vatican officials paint portrait of troubled young man with deep professional grudge, others who knew him are struggling to match image with their own more banal impressions of 23-year-old vice corporal (M)

1998-05-06.title: Gun That Looks Like Key Chain Fools Security X-Rays at Airports
1998-05-06.abstract: Interpol reports new miniature pistol that resembles key ring, that costs $20 and can fire two 32-caliber cartridges, is slipping undetected through some airport metal detectors and X-ray machines and is readily available in southern Europe; two have been seized at airport in Athens and others at airports in England and Australia; weapon is made of metal but is likely to pass through airport security checks because passengers typically put key rings, coins and other metal objects in tray that is passed around detector; Interpol says even if weapon is passed through detector, it is not likely to set of alarm unless machine is set to high level of sensitivity; diagram (M)

1998-05-06.title: Gray Eminence in U.S., Now Bright Star for Irish
1998-05-06.abstract: Interview with former US Sen George J Mitchell, who brokered peace agreement between Catholics and Protestants in Northern Ireland and has won respect and adulation of Irish; photo (M)

1998-05-07.title: Sudanese And Rebels Call for Vote In the South
1998-05-07.abstract: Peace talks between Sudanese Government and southern rebels end in Nairobi, Kenya, on note of progress, as both sides agree to accept internationally supervised vote on self-determination for southern half of nation (M)

1998-05-07.title: Chatichai Choonhavan, 76, Ex-Prime Minister of Thailand
1998-05-07.abstract: Chatichai Choonhavan, former Prime Minister of Thailand, dies at age 76; photo (M)

1998-05-07.title: COLD TRAIL: A special report.; Congo Not Alone in Ending Massacre Inquiry
1998-05-07.abstract: Howard W French special report on failure of United Nations investigation to establish facts in one of most troubling mysteries of Congo's seven-month civil war: disappearance and presumed killings of tens of thousands of Hutu refugees; refugees disappeared as they were being driven clear across Africa's third-largest country by rebel army of Laurent Kabila in his successful effort to overthrow dictator Mobutu Sese Seko; UN puts toll at 180,000 people; failure of inquiry was fault of many--not just Congolese; though Kabila pledged to cooperate, Congolese used every tactic to frustrate inquiry; UN made crucial concessions to Kabila's Government that weakened inquiry; Congo's neighbors, particularly Rwanda and Uganda, that sponsored Kabila's rebellion were hostile to any investigation that might disclose details of their involvement; Western nations, led by US, were eager to cement their ties to new authorities in Kinshasa and pressed UN to give in to Congolese demands; map; photo (M)

1998-05-07.title: Risky Task In Indonesia: Cleaning Up Banks' Mess
1998-05-07.abstract: Indonesian Government finds itself on increasingly perilous course after three days of riots and protests by students and workers; it has little choice but to reform economy, but with each step it risks alienating another segment of society; Indonesia must restructure its shattered banking system to fulfull commitments to International Monetary Fund; many of these institutions are owned by Pres Suharto's friends and business partners of his family (M)

1998-05-07.title: Mideast Turnabout
1998-05-07.abstract: Lack of tangible progress at latest round of US-Israeli-Palestinian talks in London raised few eyebrows after so many false starts over past 16 months; what may have been more significant was that Clinton Administration was no longer self-effacing 'facilitator,' but full-fledged negotiator--sitting opposite Israel; shift was evident in defiant tone with which Prime Min Benjamin Netanyahu treated US demand that Israel withdraw from 13 percent of West Bank as minimum price for reviving peace talks in Washington (M)

1998-05-08.title: Europe Turning To U.S. to Fight Illicit Cigarettes
1998-05-08.abstract: European govts, increasingly frustrated by rising volume of American cigarettes smuggled into Europe, decide to turn to Washington for help in investigating suspected involvement of American tobacco companies, starting with R J Reynolds Tobacco Co; it will be first time for Eureopean Union to formally request help from United States Govt in combatting illicit cigarette trade, and arises out of a conviction that American tobacco companies are in complicity (M)

1998-05-08.title: Annan Given Cold Shoulder By Officials In Rwanda
1998-05-08.abstract: Rwandan leaders give hostile reception to United Nations Sec Gen Kofi Annan, who visits Rwanda on what he calls 'healing mission,' and admits to Parliament that UN had done too little to stop 1994 massacres of Tutsi civilians; just before Annan's speech, Foreign Min Anastase Gasana embarrasses Annan with 10-point indictment of UN's role in country's history, culminating in killing of hundreds of thousands of people four years ago (M)

1998-05-08.title: Dark Underside of Polish Family Life: Violence
1998-05-08.abstract: Domestic violence is becoming a public issue in Poland, nation where sanctity of the family is of almost mythical importance and where most homes are organized along strict patriarchal; often quoted Polish proverb says 'If a man does not beat his wife, her liver rots'; women's groups are now discussing issue of domestic violence, holding 'tribunals' where women tell their stories and seminars to sensitize police and prosecutors, who have often dismissed their complaints; a national billboard campaign alerts Poles to idea that brutality is more than just a family spat; one poster shows badly beaten woman, and says, simply, 'Because the soup was too salty'; statistics are notoriously hard to pin down in Poland, but in 1995 the courts recorded 33 percent rise in number of domestic vilence cases, suggesting that more women are reporting such incidents; photos; map (L)

1998-05-08.title: Aides Disavow Mrs. Clinton on Mideast
1998-05-08.abstract: White House aides disown comments by Hillary Rodham Clinton about need for Palestinian state and insist that she was speaking only for herself; Mrs Clinton's comments come when she tells group of Arab and Israeli teen-agers that creating a state of Israel is 'very important for the broader goal of peace in the Middle East;' Clinton speaks via satellite to teenagers meeting in Villars, Switzerland, as part of program called Seeds for Peace; photo of Mrs Clinton and Pres Clinton (M)

1998-05-08.title: Tottenham Journal; The Cross They Carry Is the Red Cross's Blunder
1998-05-08.abstract: Thousands of Canadians received tainted blood from Canadian Red Cross in 1980's, and now red cross has become symbol of disillusionment, painful reminder of how badly their faith in the compassion of Canada has become; late last year an independent commission reported that Canada's blood system has failed to protect sick Canadians who received blood in 1980's; commission concluded that Govt had not adequately supervised Red Cross; agency has been stripped of its responsibility for blood system, and compensation was offered to about 1,000 people with AIDS; but some 42,000 Canadians contracted hepatitis C, which attacks liver and can be fatal; worried about setting too wide a precedent for compensation, federal and provincial authorities jointly set up compensation fund worth about $750 million; but fund was for benefit only of those who contracted diseasee between 1986 and 1990, when screening test was used in United States, but ignored in Canada; compensation package has become huge moral and political issue; photo (L)

1998-05-09.title: U.S. Dispatches Its Balkans Mediator With a Warning to the Serbs
1998-05-09.abstract: Pres Clinton to send mediator Richard C Holbrooke and special representative Robert S Gelbard to Belgrade to press Pres Slobodan Milosevic to end fighting in Kosovo, where ethnic Albanian majority seeks greater autonomy from Serbia (S)

1998-05-09.title: U.S. Backs Indonesian Loans But Cancels Military Exercise
1998-05-09.abstract: United States, continuing to send seemingly conflicting signals to Pres Suharto, gives Indonesia $1 billion in loan guarantees but cancels joint training exercise with Indonesian military; has declined to link aid to ease economic crisis with warnings against repression of dissidents; Export-Import Bank president James Harmon says loan package is aimed at contributing to stability and easing social unrest; students burn effigy of Suharto, Jakarta; photos (M)

1998-05-09.title: Rugby in South Africa Scrums to a New Boycott
1998-05-09.abstract: South Africa's National Sports Council seeks international boycott of nation's rugby league after league president Louis Luyt refuses to resign or apologize for humiliating Pres Nelson Mandela; Luyt has angered many in nation and sport by making Mandela defend self for two days in court during Luyt's successful legal battle to halt probe by Sports Min Steve Tshwete of charges that league practices are corrupt and racist (M)

1998-05-09.title: Bishop's Death Shakes Hopes for Guatemala Peace
1998-05-09.abstract: Recent beating death of Bishop Juan Jose Gerardi has shaken Guatemalans' confidence that long civil war is really over and plunged Pres Alvaro Arzu's Government into crisis; human rights and civic groups hold murder, three days after Gerardi made public exhaustive Roman Catholic Church probe of human rights abuses during civil war, was intended to thwart action against abusers by frightening survivors, witnesses and Government; reject Government stand that he was likely crime victim; map; photo (M)

1998-05-09.title: South African Court Ends Sodomy Laws
1998-05-09.abstract: Johannesburgh High Court overturns South Africa's sodomy laws, effectively declaring gay sex legal (S)

1998-05-10.title: As Filipinos Vote, Movie Idol Has a Starring Role
1998-05-10.abstract: Philippines Vice Pres Joseph P Estrada, former B-movie actor, is favorite to become next President in upcoming elections; polls show Estrada beating his chief rival, House Speaker Jose de Venecia, who is backed by Pres Fidel V Ramos; map; photo (M)5

1998-05-10.title: Ugly Reality In Rwanda
1998-05-10.abstract: Analysis of attacks by Rwandan leaders angry at United Nations for failing to intervene in 1994 genocide in Kigali, Rwanda; says UN Sec Gen Kofi Annan took political beating to protect Western nations, particuarly US, which heard early warnings of slaughter but resisted getting involved until it was too late; holds if outside world deserves blame, discord during Annan's visit highlights another ugly reality: It is convenient for Government to blame rest of the world for failing to save Rwanda from itself (M)

1998-05-10.title: U.S. Envoy, Again in Israel, Sees Netanyahu and Arafat
1998-05-10.abstract: Israeli and American officials acknowledge that Israeli-Palestinian summit meeting offered by Pres Clinton cannot be held on May 11, but they indicate that it will still be held, perhaps in late May; Dennis Ross, American mediator in Middle East, holds separate meetings with Yasir Arafat, Chairman of Palestinian Authority, and Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu; Ross, after meeting with Arafat, says differences that remain are not large (M)

1998-05-10.title: West Puts Pressure on Serbs With a Ban on Investment
1998-05-10.abstract: Foreign and Finance Ministers of Group of Eight countries, meeting in London as part of summit meeting in Birmingham next week, impose investment ban on Serbia and freeze its assets abroad as their concern grows about violence in Kosovo; they also freeze overseas assets of Yugolsavia, which is made up of Serbia and Montenegro; because Yugoslavia's leader Slobodan Milosevic has not met conditions set by West to withdraw special police forces from Kosovo and begin negotiations, sanctions are approved by all countries except Russia and Japan (M)

1998-05-10.title: Third World Fills a Void As Villagers Run Schools
1998-05-10.abstract: In some of world's poorest nations, villagers who are themselves often illiterate yet who understand their children need education to succeed in life are beginning to make schooling a community effort and priority for spending what small amounts of money they are able to pool; these efforts are not popular with large organizations actively involved in third-world development; Carol Bellamy, executive director of Unicef, told mothers and teachers in Afghanistan who had been hoping for more help with their home schools that providing education was responsibility of government; her view is widely shared at World Bank and in many government aid agencies (M)

1998-05-11.title: Rugby Chief Quits, Averting South Africa Boycott
1998-05-11.abstract: Luis Luyt, president of South African Rugby Football Assn, reverses decision and will resign, amid call by National Sport Council's call for international boycott of South African rugby; governing body for sports had demanded that entire executive board of rugby quit, that it apologize to Pres Nelson Mandela for taking him to court earlier in year, and that it cooperate with investigation into charges of racism and corruption in rugby (S)

1998-05-11.title: Valladolid Journal; Early Bird Begins Mexico's 2000 Presidential Race
1998-05-11.abstract: Interview with Vicente Fox Quesada Fox, Governor of Guanajuato, Mex, who is campaigning for Presidency, even though election is in 2000; photo; map (M)

1998-05-11.title: Ruling Party In Paraguay Says It Won; Fraud Alleged
1998-05-11.abstract: Paraguay's governing party claims victory in Presidential elections, with only heavily contested exit polls available and electoral officials complaining of considerable fraud, creating momentum for celebrating among party faithful in advance of official tallies (M)

1998-05-11.title: Road to Riches Starts In the Golden Triangle
1998-05-11.abstract: Interview with Lo Hsing-han, former opium warlord of Golden Triangle of 1960's who currently has reputation as one of Myanmar's most influential businessmen; US views has prosperity as evidence that Myanmar's economy is awash in laundered drug money and that it military leaders have encouraged those who trade in drugs to invest in its development projects; photo (M)

1998-05-11.title: Italian Town Buries 90 After Mudslide
1998-05-11.abstract: Outdoor funeral is held in Sarno, Italy, for 90 victims of mudslides caused by torrential rains; photo; map (M)

1998-05-12.title: Vexed, Mexico Deports 40 in Italian Rights Group
1998-05-12.abstract: Mexican Govt deports 40 members of Italian delegation of human rights observers who clashed repeatedly with authorities during weeklong tour through conflict-torn Chiapas state; enacted tough Constitutional clause that allows President Ernesto Zedillo to expel foreigners deemed undesirable; photo (S)

1998-05-12.title: TESTS BRING A SHARP OUTCRY
1998-05-12.abstract: India conducts three underground nuclear tests at its Pokharan desert testing range in Rajasthan state, nearly 24 years after detonating only nuclear device; move seemingly signals determination to abandon decades of ambiguity regarding possession of nuclear weapons; Prime Min Atal Bihari Vajpayee, leader of Hindu nationalist party that has advocated embrace of nuclear weapons as step toward great-power status, makes announcement congratulating scientists and engineers; spokesman says tests will help scientists design variety of nuclear weapons for fast-developing missile program; tests are widely welcomed in India, with hardly any immediate dissent from opposition political parties and little sign of Ghandian pacifism that was strong element in policy in early years after independence; are condemned by international community; Clinton Administration expresses deep disappointment; is reviewing trade and financial sanctions under US nuclear nonproliferation laws; Britain voices dismay; Germany calls tests 'slap in the face' for 149 countries that signed nuclear nonproliferation treaty; UN Sec Gen Kofi Annan expresses 'deep regret;' Pakistan weighs nuclear test of its own, which would be its first; blames Western nations, particularly US, for failing to heed its recent warnings about nuclear ambitions of Vajpayee Govt; map; photos (M)

1998-05-12.title: Ruling Party in Paraguay Maintains Its Long Grip on Presidency
1998-05-12.abstract: Paraguay's governing Colorado Party sweeps presidential elections, defeating coalition of opposition parties to maintain its half-century-long hold on power (M)

1998-05-12.title: Albright Gets New Mission: Patch Rift With Israel
1998-05-12.abstract: Pres Clinton, risking more signs of friction in his Mideast policy, orders Sec of State Madeleine Albright to meet with Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu of Israel in Washington on May 13 to resolve latest impasse in peace talks; move, also aimed at easing US-Israeli tensions, follows failure of talks in London to seal agreement with Netanyahu on scope of further pullbacks from West Bank in return for stronger Palestinian moves against Muslim militants; photo (M)

1998-05-12.title: Irish Accord Proves To Be Trickier Sale Than First Imagined
1998-05-12.abstract: Difficulty of creating advertising theme for Northern Ireland peace accord demonstrates delicate political and emotional undercurrents that have come to fore as Catholics and Protestants intensify campaigns for or against it before May 22 referendum; several leaders who favor pact acknowledge that persuading people to back it has been more complicated than previously imagined; Irish are being asked to approve something that arouses apprehension among all voters; very notion of political campaign to resolve decades of seething hatreds seems alien to place where differences have long been settled with violence; residents of Belfast are so suspicious of promises of peace that political parties are finding little enthusiasm for referendum; photo (M)

1998-05-13.title: Spy Agencies Under Attack for Providing No Warning : Clinton Vows to Punish India
1998-05-13.abstract: WASHINGTON—  President Bill Clinton vowed Tuesday to apply U.S. laws imposing strong sanctions against India after its underground nuclear tests, and he implored other countries in the region not to follow India "down the path of a dangerous arms race."

1998-05-13.title: Indian's Letter to Clinton On the Nuclear Testing
1998-05-13.abstract: Text of letter from Prime Min Atal Bihari Vajpayee to Pres Clinton explaining rationale for India's nuclear tests (S)

1998-05-13.title: China Is 'Seriously Concerned' But Restrained in Its Criticism
1998-05-13.abstract: China condemns India's underground nuclear tests with notable brevity and restraint (S)

1998-05-13.title: Britain to Spend $520 Million to Pep Up Ulster
1998-05-13.abstract: Britain proposes $520 million aid package for Northern Ireland as part of effort to win voter approval of peace accord (S)

1998-05-13.title: Sharon Won't Join Netanyahu in Meeting Albright
1998-05-13.abstract: Min Ariel Sharon, who is considered key to Israeli Government approval of any peace deal, says he will not join Prime Min Benjamin Netanyahu in his meeting with State Sec Madeleine K Albright; protests Netanyahu's unauthorized 'promises'; stand will stiffen Netanyahu's resistance to United States pressure for further withdrawal from West Bank (M)

1998-05-14.title: Next-Door Neighbor Demands That World Powers Shun India
1998-05-14.abstract: Pakistani leaders demand United States and other world powers act decisively to isolate India after its five nuclear tests; strongly hint that strength of international response will shape their decision on whether to detonate own nuclear bombs; Min Gohar Ayub Khan holds invoking of mandatory sanctions under US law is not effective response (M)

1998-05-14.title: INDIA CARRIES OUT 2 MORE ATOM TESTS DESPITE SANCTIONS
1998-05-14.abstract: India, ignoring appeal from Pres Clinton, carries out two more underground nuclear tests; Government of Prime Min Atal Bihari Vajpayee approved tests in face of threatened economic sanctions; Clinton announces wide-ranging sanctions, including end to most United States aid and threat to use US vote to deny India International Monetary Fund and World Bank loans, news conference, Berlin; Japan and number of other nations halt, cut or defer aid; map; photo (L)

1998-05-14.title: Floods Giving Argentine President a Political Lift
1998-05-14.abstract: Flooding in Argentina caused by El Nino has forced 100,000 people from their homes and caused more than $1.5 billion in damage since it began in Apr; Pres Carlos Saul Menem has seized opportunity to spur flagging popularity, traveling to many stricken areas to buoy his image as take-charge leader; his backers seek Constitutional change to allow him to run for third term; photo (M)

1998-05-14.title: Kremlin Soft-Pedals Its Rebuke to India, and Opposes Sanctions
1998-05-14.abstract: Min Yevgeny M Primakov reaffirms Russia's refusal to join other countries in economic sanctions to punish India for resuming nuclear tests; Russia'a complaints against testing have been muted, reflecting its close commercial ties with India and view of India as counterweight to China (M)

1998-05-14.title: Clinton Calls Tests a 'Terrible Mistake' And Announces Sanctions Against India
1998-05-14.abstract: Pres Clinton, visiting Germany, sharpens his criticism of Indian Government, calling five underground nuclear tests 'terrible mistake;' imposes raft of economic sanctions; House Speaker Newt Gingrich scores Clinton for punishing India while overlooking China's sale of missile technology to Pakistan and Iran; Sen Jesse Helms holds India constitutes emerging nuclear threat to United States; other members of Congress generally score India, back Clinton's move and deplore US failure to anticipate tests; Chancellor Helmut Kohl does not immediately set sanctions, but says Germany will clearly oppose tests; photo (M)

1998-05-15.title: Role of Military: A Potential Unifier Split by Conflicting Goals
1998-05-15.abstract: Key to Indonesia's future lies increasingly in hands of its powerful military, as nation suffers its worst crisis in 30 years, its capital seething with rage and Pres Suharto struggling to keep control; the one institution with an efficient nationwide orgn, and one institution that enjoys broad support among most Indonesians, may ultimately decide whether it is time for Suharto to heed widespread calls to step aside afer 32 years in power; Suharto has worked hard to insure loyalty of military, appointing his supporters to all top positions (News analysis) (M)

1998-05-15.title: House Votes to Bar Religious Abuses Abroad
1998-05-15.abstract: House overwhelmingly approves bill to punish foreign countries that persecute people for their religious beliefs; lopsided vote, 375 to 41, masks many lawmakers' ambivalence toward a popular bill that aims to champion religious liberties worldwide, but which critics say will imperil religious minorities, create hierarchy of human rights violations, and hurt United States foreign policy; under bill, countries that permit or endorse pattern of violent attacks or other persecution of religious believers would automatically face cutoff of United States aid, other than food and medical supplies, restrictions on exports and American opposition to loans by international financial institutions, including International Monetary Fund; among countries criticized in debate are China, Pakistan, Indonesia and Sudan; bill is unlikely to be adopted by Senate, which is expected to pass a measure giving President more flexibility in dealing with nations accused of religious persecution (M)

1998-05-15.title: Tension? What Tension? Not on a Smiley Day for Netanyahu.
1998-05-15.abstract: Israeli Prime Min Benjamin Netanyahu gets warm, bipartisan reception on his visit to Washington, in hallways and lunchrooms of Congress; through day of courtesy calls, fresh fruit and speechmaking, Netanyahu is welcomed with kind of full-throated, bipartisan heartiness that he rarely receives at home (M)

1998-05-15.title: Helms Plans $100 Million Aid Package for Cuba
1998-05-15.abstract: Sen Jesse Helms, chairman of Senate Foreign Relations Committee, proposes $100 million aid package for Cuba that he contends will improve lives of Cubans while weakening hold of Fidel Castro; proposal, which is endorsed by more than 20 others Senators from both parties, would channel food and medical assistance to Cuban people through Roman Catholic Church and politically independent relief organizations (S)

1998-05-15.title: U.S. Talks With Israel On Mideast Move Slowly
1998-05-15.abstract: State Sec Madeleine K Albright and Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu of Israel end second round of talks in Washington without achieving breakthrough on reviving stalled Middle East peace negotiations; but senior American and Israeli officials say that lower-level aides from each side will continue working on issues on Friday and over weekend (M)

1998-05-16.title: Huitzilac Journal; A Long Fire Season Torments Mexico
1998-05-16.abstract: Burning season in Mexico is lasting far longer than usual amid drought, with nearly 10,000 forest and brush fires having claimed scores of lives, forced airport closings and left at least 50 million Mexicans choking in smoke that covers thousands of square miles; Mexico City declares environmental alert because of smoke, urging residents to stay inside; fires are also burning out of control in Guatemala and Honduras; Mexico says it will accept fire-fighting aid offered by United States; map; photos (M)

1998-05-16.title: SUHARTO REVERSES HIKE IN FUEL PRICE DEMANDED BY I.M.F.
1998-05-16.abstract: Indonesian Pres Suharto, responding to rioting, returns hurriedly from Cairo and rolls back new fuel-price rises imposed at International Monetary Fund insistence; confers with advisers and top generals as calls for his ouster grow on streets and from public figures; tanks guard suddenly quiet streets of Jakarta and Presidential Palace; young looters still roam streets; more rioting is reported in Jogjakarta, Solo and Ugung Pandang; Lt Gen Prabowo, Suharto's son-in-law, says there are no splits in military; ethnic Chinese citizens, whose homes and stores have come under attack, take refuge in hotels or gather at airports seeking to flee nation; foreigners also throng to airports after American Embassy advises United States citizens to leave; embassy plans charter flights to evacuate Americans; other nations also prepare to evacuate citizens; Pres Clinton says Indonesians must decide own political future; calls for reform; map; photo (M)

1998-05-16.title: Central Bank In Indonesia On Sidelines; Worry Grows
1998-05-16.abstract: Indonesia's central bank again stops acting as middleman in foreign-currency transactions, further weakening confidence in battered economy; outlook for Indonesian currency, rupiah, becomes murkier (M)

1998-05-16.title: New Delhi Premier Indicates Resolve to Produce Nuclear Weapons
1998-05-16.abstract: Prime Min Atal Bihari Vajpayee indicates that India will not hesitate to deploy nuclear arms, magazine interview; Pres Clinton, at Birmingham, Eng, economic summit meeting, wins accord on firm statement criticizing India, but fails to mobilize other nations to take punitive action; photos (M)

1998-05-16.title: The Target Of Violence In a Time Of Wrath
1998-05-16.abstract: Violence against Indonesia's ethnic Chinese residents, who make up about 5 percent of population but own 75 percent of nation's wealth, has been ugly sideshow in every Indonesian political upheaval since colonial days; Jakarta's Chinese are fleeing or cowering in shops in wake of looting and arson by angry mobs; fear trip to airport after attacks by mobs searching cars for Chinese passengers (M)

1998-05-17.title: Brazil Congress Rejects President's Plan for Pension Changes
1998-05-17.abstract: Brazilian Congress rejects constitutional amendment to raise minimum age for retirement, dealing significant defeat to Pres Fernando Henrique Cardoso's plans for social security reforms; amendment would have required men to work until they turned 60 and to be employed for 35 years to qualify for benefits, and women to be 55 and to work for at least 30 years; labor unions and opposition parties complained that changes would have penalized unskilled laborers, who tend to enter work force at earlier ages and would be required to work more years until age 60 (M)

1998-05-17.title: On the Street, Pride and Worry Over Tests
1998-05-17.abstract: Whether Pakistan should test nuclear weapons in response to recent underground tests by India has caused intense debate among Pakistanis; many residents of Rawalpindi are ambivalent and, like their leaders, seem deeply torn by issue that could decide country's future; national pride and deep-seated fear of Indian aggression have led many to demand quick response in form of tests, but concern over devastating economic sanctions that would follow--together with recognition of country's desperate domestic needs--have led others to argue for caution; map; photo (M)

1998-05-17.title: Rich Leaders Turn Eye to Crime and Debt
1998-05-17.abstract: Leaders of eight major industrial countries attending economic summit in Birmingham, England, agree to cooperate more in battling international crime; pledge to do more to help world's poorest countries; focus on issues of common interest, including how to create more jobs and stop global warming; thousands of protestors surround site of meeting in central Birmingham, demanding more action by rich nations to relieve poorest countries of their debts; photo (M)

1998-05-17.title: Apartheid's Architects Losing White Votes in South Africa
1998-05-17.abstract: Loss of Parliamentary seats by National Party in three recent local elections is seen as first sign of possible big swing away from party that ruled South Africa from 1947 to 1994; loss is first tangible evidence that support for builder of apartheid is being lost to Democratic Party--heir to white liberal anti-apartheid parties of 1970's and 1980's (M)

1998-05-17.title: Summit Talks Shift Focus To Atom Tests By New Delhi
1998-05-17.abstract: Underground nuclear tests by India shifts focus of economic summit of eight leading industrial nations in Birmingham, England; Pres Clinton steps up criticisms of India in hopes of dissuading Pakistan from following suit; has so far had little success in persuading allies to follow US lead by imposing economic sanctions on India (M)

1998-05-18.title: Pakistan Seems Mixed On Holding Nuclear Test
1998-05-18.abstract: Foreign Min Gohar Ayub Khan says Pakistan has decided to begin testing nuclear weapons in response to recent tests by India, but other Government officials are less clear on whether decision has been made (M)

1998-05-18.title: Insurers Swindled Jews, Nazi Files Show
1998-05-18.abstract: Fresh details surrounding Kristallnacht, outburst of anti-Semitic violence in Germany that presaged Holocaust, have been extracted from Government archives in Moscow that have been storing Nazi documents seized by Soviet troops in 1945; newly unearthed documents suggest German insurance companies not only connived with Nazis to avoid paying Jewish policyholders for damages to synagogues and thousands of Jewish shops and homes, but sometimes also exceeded Government guidelines and canceled all policies on which Jews had been paying premiums for years (M)

1998-05-18.title: Clinton Seeks to Limit Effects of India's Test by Pressing Yeltsin on Arms Pact
1998-05-18.abstract: Pres Clinton, on final day of Birmingham, Eng, meeting of leaders of world's eight leading industrial nations, urges Russian Pres Boris Yeltsin to press for ratification of pending nuclear weapons treaty even as US and allies search for way to head off nuclear arms race between India and Pakistan; fails to win broad support for economic sanctions against India; says India is swimming against currents of history (M)

1998-05-18.title: The Group of Seven Now Eight, Officially
1998-05-18.abstract: Annual summit of world's seven major industrial democracies and Russia become formally known as Group of Eight as full recognition is finally given to Russia (S)

1998-05-18.title: To Some of Indonesia's Poor, Looting Gives Taste of Power
1998-05-18.abstract: Rioting and looting in Indonesia, often described as spasm of anti-Government violence, has more complex motivations that will not be easily remedied, even if Indonesia becomes completely democratic; underscore emergence of volatile underclass in cities like Jakarta, including people who came to city during country's economic boom but now find themselves unemployed; many are embittered that they must struggle to survive while wealthy continue living good life; see their thievery as kind of Robin Hood-style self-help program, and say they are ready to loot again in absence of change; rioting has caused more then 500 deaths and damage to more than 4,900 buildings (M)

1998-05-19.title: China May Give Pakistan Defense Assurances, Averting A-Tests
1998-05-19.abstract: High-level Pakistani delegation arrives, Beijing; analysts say China might give Pakistan defense assurances it needs to refrain from responding to India's recent nuclear tests with own tests (M)

1998-05-19.title: Kohl Paints Opponent As Far Leftist
1998-05-19.abstract: German Chancellor Helmut Kohl opens re-election drive with harsh, personal attack on main challenger, accusing Gerhard Schroder of steering land toward 'leftist republic,' speech to special meeting of his Christian Democratic Union; scores him for allowing political accord with Communists in Saxony-Anhalt; photo (M)

1998-05-19.title: In a Suharto Fief, 'Hang Suharto!'
1998-05-19.abstract: Thousands of Indonesia college students stage anti-Suharto demonstration outside Parliament, Jakarta; military lets students from dozens of schools enter building to air their grievances in meetings with Government representatives; photo (M)

1998-05-19.title: Rumors of Mideast Deal Bring Denials, Fueling More Rumors
1998-05-19.abstract: Prime Min Benjamin Netanyahu and American officials deny reports that Israel had agreed to 13 percent withdrawal from West Bank sought by Clinton Administration; denials spur speculation that real progress is being made and that officials are being extremely careful not to discuss it; State Sec Madeleine K Albright meets with Palestinian leader Yasir Arafat, London (M)

1998-05-19.title: High Stakes for Suharto: No Graceful Way Out?
1998-05-19.abstract: Analysis of Indonesian Pres Suharto's narrowing options in face of mounting demands for his resignation; he may believe that he must crush opposition or risk loss of power and of his family's vast corporate empire; military effort could cause bloodshed, rifts in armed forces and great uncertainty for nation (M)

1998-05-20.title: Mexicans Belittle Drug-Money Sting
1998-05-20.abstract: Mexican officials seek to belittle results of huge United States undercover operation that exposed laundering of drug money in 12 Mexican banks after Federal grand jury in Los Angeles unsealed three indictments against Mexican bankers and their clients in drug organizations based in Ciudad Juarez and in Cali, Colombia; more than $72 million in drug proceeds was seized during three-year undercover operation (M)

1998-05-20.title: A Country's Commerce Is Paralyzed and a Food Shortage Looms in the Capital
1998-05-20.abstract: Indonesia's economy remains paralyzed and country drifts into political limbo after four days of rioting and after Pres Suharto rebuffs demands for immediate resignation (M)

1998-05-20.title: U.N. Gives Its First Grants From Big Ted Turner Gift
1998-05-20.abstract: First round of grants from Ted Turner's $1 billion gift to UN will be announced on May 20, with nearly three-quarters of the initial $22 million going to help women, children and victims of war in poor countries; recipients of largest grants--and more than half of initial $22 million--will be UN Population Fund and UN Children's Fund; Turner fund president Timothy E Wirth comments (M)

1998-05-20.title: Suharto's Stealthy Foe: Globalizing Capitalism
1998-05-20.abstract: News analysis of political turmoil in Indonesia and its portents for Government of Pres Suharto and of other countries in region; capitalism, markets and globalization--and not Communist conspiracy--caused caused current crisis, and tumult underscores way financial crisis is reshaping Asian politics and society as well as business (M)

1998-05-20.title: ARMY OUT IN FORCE TO HALT PROTESTS AIMED AT SUHARTO
1998-05-20.abstract: Thousands of anti-Government protestors gather outside Parliament building in Jakarta, Indonesia, chanting for removal of Pres Suharto, despite massive show of military force aimed at preventing demonstration; relations between protestors and troops appear friendly; cheering students escort two truckloads of soldiers onto grounds; Parliament, which has been occupied by students for three days, has become focal point of nation's defiance of Pres Suharto, who has rejected widespread calls for resignation while still promising new national elections; photos (M)

1998-05-21.title: Joy and Anger Erupt at Main Symbol of Government
1998-05-21.abstract: Students occupying Indonesian Parliament building, Jakarta, hail Pres Suharto's resignation; call on Parliament to replace Suharto's chosen successor, Vice Pres B J Habibie (M)

1998-05-21.title: Art Thieves in Rome Take 2 Van Goghs and a Cezanne
1998-05-21.abstract: Three armed men hide in National Gallery of Modern Art, Rome, after closing time, tie up guards and steal two paintings by Van Gogh and one by Cezanne; theft shocks Italian art world and ripples through political and law-enforcement circles; photos (M)

1998-05-21.title: Visiting Nuclear Site, Indian Leader Puts Pakistan on Notice
1998-05-21.abstract: Prime Min Atal Bihari Vajpayee visits site of India's nuclear tests; warns Pakistan to adopt more conciliatory attitude that recognizes India's newly enhanced might; Min Lal Krishna Advani earlier warned Pakistan to ease pressure on India from Pakistani-backed insurgents in Kashmir (M)

1998-05-21.title: After the Web's Arrival, the Deluge of Log-Ons : On-Line Identity Crisis:Seeking a Unique Name
1998-05-21.abstract: NEW YORK—  "Please choose a log-on name." Five words that strike terror, or at least ennui, into the heart of your average Netizen. Even before the Internet allowed consumers to deal directly with businesses, there were identities and passwords for mainframes at work, automated teller machines and home and office security systems.

1998-05-21.title: Congo Leader Losing Luster
1998-05-21.abstract: Congo Pres Laurent Kabila, year after ending corrupt rule of Mobutu Sese Seko, has failed to reverse years of poverty and decline in former Zaire; cross-border violence continues, mining investment lags and Kabila increasingly uses repressive tactics of his predecessor, dimming democratic hopes; map; photos (M)

1998-05-22.title: THE FALL OF SUHARTO
1998-05-22.abstract: MILITARY POWER BROKERS -- The army has a low profile, but great influence. Page A9.    A MIXED LEGACY -- Suharto fostered strong economic growth, and staggering graft. Page A9.

1998-05-22.title: In Bulgaria, Students Bone Up on Pax Americana
1998-05-22.abstract: BLAGOEVGRAD, Bulgaria—  They waited through the valedictorian's oration, through the honorary degree for a minor poet, through an address by a distinguished foreign ambassador, until finally the moment came when the provost pronounced them "Bachelors of Arts." Tasseled mortarboards were tossed into the air, only to be carefully retrieved for family photographs.

1998-05-22.title: Clinton Agrees to Land-Mine Ban, but Not Yet
1998-05-22.abstract: Clinton Administration, in policy shift, pledges that by year 2006 US will sign international treaty banning anti-personnel land mines, but only if Pentagon comes up with alternative weapon; letter from national security adviser Samuel Berger to Sen Patrick Leahy does not set firm deadline and, in any case, Bill Clinton will not be President by 2006; Pentagon has resisted deadline, and plays down new pledge, which emerged from showdown with Leahy over little-noticed law that imposes moratorium on use of anti-personnel mines starting February 1999; moratorium was sponsored by Leahy and became law before treaty became an issue (S)

1998-05-22.title: For Mo Mowlam, at Least, Yes for a Job Well Done
1998-05-22.abstract: Mo Mowlam, Britain's Secretary for Northern Ireland, is winner regardless of outcome of referendum on peace settlement; new poll shows approval rating of famously unceremonious, outspoken Mowlam exceeds that of Tony Blair, most popular prime minister in British history; biographical profile of 48-year-old Labor party figure from Watford, whose parents were postal workers; she was diagnosed with nonmalignant brain tumor in 1996 and underwent radiation treatment that left her temporarily bald, situation she addressed with wigs and good humor; photo(M)

1998-05-22.title: New Labour, Old Goal:Strong Workers' Unions
1998-05-22.abstract: LONDON—  The government of Prime Minister Tony Blair undertook an ambitious attempt on Thursday to roll back the labor-market deregulation of the Thatcher era by unveiling a package of proposals to guarantee a worker's right to union representation and strengthen protection against unfair dismissals.

1998-05-23.title: Gently, Indonesia's Military Ends Seizure of Parliament
1998-05-23.abstract: Indonesian army retakes Parliament in overnight raid by soldiers who prod and cajole more than 2,000 students to leave complex they occupied for five days; no casualties reported as new Government moves rapidly to assert control; photo of soldiers in impromptu dance; student movement, united against former Pres Suharto, splinters as some support successor B J Habibie, while others say new Cabinet does not go far enough to sweep out Suharto remnants (S)

1998-05-23.title: Senate Backs Bill Limiting Chemical Arms Ban
1998-05-23.abstract: Senate approves legislation that would impose tough restrictions on implementation of chemical weapons treaty that was approved last year; conditions, put forth at insistence of treaty's critics, would limit number of annual inspections, restrict location where suspect samples could be tested and give President unilateral right to block surprise inspections on national security grounds, which is not allowed under treaty; arms control agency official John D Holum says priority is getting law approved to clear way for industry inspections which are already almost year overdue; measure is approved as part of bill aimed at Iran's missile program (S)

1998-05-23.title: U.S. Companies Wary Of Their Prospects Now
1998-05-23.abstract: American businesses are wary about prospects in Indonesia under new leader B J Habibie, who is closely linked to former Pres Suharto's crony capitalism; Habibie is often described as full of energy and enthusiasm, talking rapidly and continually (S)

1998-05-23.title: Switzerland Said to Have Backed Nazi Trade in Looted Gold
1998-05-23.abstract: Declassified US documents suggest that Swiss Government itself endorsed wartime trade in looted Nazi gold and that, in at least one case, Swiss bankers deliberately sought out gold they knew to be looted; World Jewish Congress releases information as Swiss panel prepares to rule on extent of official connivance; papers reveal secret financial dealings during closing stages of World War II, and important economic ties between Nazi Germany and Switzerland; Swiss National Bank was single biggest recipient of gold sold by German central bank to help finance Hitler's war effort; quotes from papers suggesting Government officials, not just bankers, were privy to trade in looted gold (M)

1998-05-23.title: Top General Strengthens His Grasp On Power
1998-05-23.abstract: Gen Wiranto, Indonesia's most powerful military figure, consolidates his power under new Pres B J Habibie by removing son-in-law of ex-Pres Suharto from command post; Wiranto, who finally persuaded Suharto to step down, is now serving both as armed forces chief and Defense Minister; Suharto and several of his six unpopular children, grown vastly rich, are in seclusion in Jakarta; critics note Government still has same old faces; photo of student demonstrators (M)

1998-05-24.title: IRISH VOTERS, NORTH AND SOUTH, GIVE RESOUNDING 'YES' TO PEACE
1998-05-24.abstract: Northern Ireland and Irish Republic voters overwhelmingly approve Northern Ireland peace accord; voters in North, turning out in record numbers, approve accord by 71 percent to 29%, margin deemed crucial to deny opponents chance to undermine new institutions that will be set up; 94 percent vote yes in Irish Republic; British Sec Mo Mowlam and Prime Min Tony Blair hail vote; photos (M)

1998-05-24.title: Iran's Tension Underscored In Celebrating Its President
1998-05-24.abstract: A year after an election that changed the face of Iranian politics, students and ordinary Iranians rallied by the tens of thousands here today to revel in the openness instilled by the new President, Mohammad Khatami.    The anniversary gathering was the most raucous and uninhibited political demonstration seen in Iran in many years. But it came against a backdrop of sharpening tensions between the kinds of moderate Iranians who came out to celebrate Mr. Khatami's landslide victory and conservatives who view his popularity as a real threat to their long-time grip on power.

1998-05-24.title: Nicole Maxwell, a Bold Seeker Of Medical Herbs, Dies at 92
1998-05-24.abstract: Nicole Maxwell, free-spirited San Francisco debutante who plunged into remote Amazon jungles of Ecuador and Peru and came out with trove of medicinal plants that she said Indians used to cure virtually every known ailment, died on May 5 at age 92; photo (M)d

1998-05-24.title: In the End, Voters Found, a Real Chance at Peace Was Irresistible
1998-05-24.abstract: Supporters of Northern Ireland peace accord, approved by 71 percent of voters in North, succeeded in building coalition that includes majority of Catholic nationalists and large share of Protestant Unionists; convinced more than half of wavering Unionists that treaty offers chance for peace; photo (M)

1998-05-24.title: A Key Figure Says the Need For Elections Is Imperative
1998-05-24.abstract: Indonesian Pres B J Habibie swears in his new Cabinet; Economy, Finance and Industry Min Ginandjar Kartasasmita, holding Government needs popular mandate, calls for elections as soon as possible; blames nation's economic woes on political instability; students, evicted from Parliament by hundreds of troops after 5-day occupation, again rally against Habibie at Indonesia University, Jakarta; some 2,000 troops guard Parliament to bar students from returning; photo (M)/

1998-05-25.title: Big Parade in Jerusalem; Arabs Call It Big Bluster
1998-05-25.abstract: Israel stages largest military parade in over 20 years, Jerusalem, marking capture of East Jerusalem in 1967 and Israel's 50th anniversary; Palestinians decry march as provocation; Israeli critics call it jingoistic display; march steers clear of Arab areas of East Jerusalem; photos (M)5

1998-05-25.title: AMERICAN TOPICS : Some Little-Known History
1998-05-25.abstract: The soldiers in the military re-enactment wore homespun tunics, tricornered hats and carried muskets as they recreated Revolutionary War battles and life. All of them were black, yet the flag over their camp was the Union Jack.

1998-05-25.title: Bogota Halts Unit Faulted Over Rights
1998-05-25.abstract: Colombian army intelligence brigade suspected of links to political killings will be disbanded (S)

1998-05-25.title: 2 Czech Cities to Wall Off Their 'Problematic' Gypsies
1998-05-25.abstract: USTI NAD LABEM, Czech Republic—  Two Czech cities have decided to fence in what they call "problematic" public housing residents, creating what is virtually a ghetto for the residents, mainly Gypsies, who officials say ruin the calm, orderly life of their neighbors.

1998-05-25.title: U.S. May Have Helped India Hide Its Nuclear Activity
1998-05-25.abstract: Some officials say spy satellite photos then United States Amb Frank Wisner Jr showed to top Indian officials in successful 1995 effort to get them to stop preparations for nuclear tests showed Indians ways to conceal their preparations this time; failure of US intelligence and diplomatic officials to detect latest preparations discussed; photo (M)h

1998-05-26.title: SOWING DEATH: A special report.; How Japan Germ Terror Alerted World
1998-05-26.abstract: Court testimony by member of Japan's Aum Shinrikyo religious cult shows it carried out repeated germ attacks in early 1990's to kill millions of people throughout Tokyo and at nearby American bases where thousands of service people and their families live; group sprayed pestilential microbes and germ toxins from rooftops and convoys of trucks, hoping to ignite apocalyptic war; its targets included Japanese Diet, Imperial Palace, surrounding city and American base at Yokohama, which is headquarters of Seventh Fleet; at least nine attacks failed, largely because group never possessed germs of sufficient virulence, despite great effort; cult sought lethal bacteria from local sources and traveled on microbe-hunting trips to a northern Japanese isle as well as to Africa, apparently eager to obtain deaded Ebola virus; burst into headlines in 1995 when it released nerve gas into Tokyo subways, killing dozen people; its biological work, meant to be thousand times more devastating, was mentioned only in passing, in scattered reports; photos; map (L)

1998-05-26.title: Toxic Sludge Cleanup to Cost Spain 100 Million
1998-05-26.abstract: Spanish Government says toxic sludge that burst from mine reservoir and flooded rivers and farms in southern Spain in April will take more than $100 million to clean up; map (M)

1998-05-26.title: In Revenge, Jews Build In Muslim Part Of Old City
1998-05-26.abstract: Jewish settlers pour foundations for new houses in Muslim Quarter of Jerusalem's walled Old City, expanding their foothold in Arab neighborhood in revenge for fatal stabbing of settler earlier in month; appear to be working without building permit from city government, which periodically demolishes illegally built Arab houses in East Jerusalem; photo; map (M)

1998-05-26.title: With Estrada's Victory Certain, Everybody's in His Parade
1998-05-26.abstract: MANILA—  Suddenly, shining new stickers bearing the word "Erap" or "Estrada" are appearing on the bumpers of the big vans and limousines that carry the elite of Metro Manila from their rambling homes in walled "villages" to the high-rise office buildings of Makati, the central business and financial district.

1998-05-26.title: San Juan Ozololtepec Journal; In the Hills, Marijuana Fields and a Priest's Murder
1998-05-26.abstract: Unsolved murder of Rev Mauro Ortiz Carreno, local parish priest in San Juan Ozololtepec, Mexico, is believed to be work of local drug traffickers, whom he denounced; church officials scoff at official explanation that murder was committed by a man to whose former wife Ortiz lent $1,250; map; photo (M)

1998-05-27.title: Wuhan Journal; Selling in China: A Smile, a Shoeshine and a Scam
1998-05-27.abstract: China, responding to scams in which would-be door-to-door salesmen spent heavily on unsaleable goods, has banned all direct selling; ban led sales companies to close abruptly, provoking riots in Wuhan and other cities where salespeople were unable to return unsold goods; ban has been disaster for American companies like Amway, Avon Products and Mary Kay, which have invested in sales networks; photo (M)

1998-05-27.title: Hong Kong Confronts Democracy: How Soon?
1998-05-27.abstract: Hong Kong's pro-democracy politicians, emboldened by their sweeping election victory, are calling for swifter transition to full democracy; Democratic Party head Martin Lee calls for direct election of all 60 members of legislature by 2000 and of chief executive by 2002 (M)

1998-05-27.title: Fighting Breaks Out in Georgia Buffer Region
1998-05-27.abstract: Flareup in five-year-old conflict over Black Sea region of Abkhazia forces Georgian Pres Eduard Shevardnadze to accept wave of tens of thousands of refugees today and to cancel Independence Day celebrations; fighting between Abkhazian separatists and Georgian militias has erupted in buffer area between breakaway Republic of Abkhazia and Georgian-held territory which is policed by Russian battalion assigned by Commonwealth of Independent States to keep peace; map (M)

1998-05-27.title: Italian Officials Seek Charges in Ski Lift Case
1998-05-27.abstract: Italian prosecutors seek manslaughter indictments against four-member crew of United States Marine Corps jet that slice through ski lift cables in Feb, killing 20 people, and against crew's commanding officers; crew faces court-martial in US, which claimed jurisdiction under NATO charter; prosecutors' apparently hope to try defendants in absentia (M)

1998-05-27.title: I.M.F. Assesses New Indonesia Government
1998-05-27.abstract: High-level International Monetary Fund team visits Indonesia to explore ways to restart rescue package in face of political upheaval that has worsened severe economic problems; Hubert Neiss, who heads team, cites need for political stability; photo (M)

1998-05-28.title: MOSCOW ATTEMPTS DRASTIC MEASURES TO SAVE ECONOMY
1998-05-28.abstract: Russia mounts drastic effort to preserve value of its currency and stem exodus of foreign investors as its financial markets teeter on brink of collapse; officials triple interest rates, announce that they will be willing to accept lower price for selloff of Russia's last big oil company, Rosneft, and appeal for support from International Monetary Fund; Moscow's financial markets have been under siege as fears have mounted among investors and US officials that economic turmoil in Asia will spread to Russia; Moscow stock market is world's worst performing market, off more than 50 percent this year; swoon in market has turned into profound political crisis and has become major worry for US policymakers who have thrown their support behind Pres Boris N Yeltsin's Government; graph shows plunge in Russian stock market and rise in interest rates (M)

1998-05-28.title: It's Serb vs. Serb in Montenegro Vote
1998-05-28.abstract: Article on coming parliamentary elections in Montenegro notes that candidates who support Pres Milo Djukanovic are opposed by followers of Yugoslav Pres Slobodan Milosevic; map; photo of Djukanovic supporters at rally (M)

1998-05-28.title: 4 Colombians Pledge Change but Carry Lots of Old Baggage
1998-05-28.abstract: Change has become byword of four candidates who hope to replace Colombian Pres Ernesto Samper in coming elections; all four have past associations that undermine their promise to transform country; candidates are Andres Pastrana, Horacio Serpa, Noemi Sanin and Harold Bedoya (M)

1998-05-28.title: U.S., Australia and Portugal Call for the Release of Resistance Leader : Jakarta Under Pressure on East Timor
1998-05-28.abstract: JAKARTA—  Indonesia came under mounting pressure Wednesday to resolve one of its most contentious foreign policy problems — East Timor — as the United States, Portugal and Australia called for the release of the imprisoned East Timorese resistance leader to hasten a settlement.

1998-05-28.title: Iraqi Leader's Daughter Tries Lawsuit to Seek Smuggled Millions
1998-05-28.abstract: Rana Kamel, daughter of Iraqi Pres Saddam Hussein, begins legal action in Amman, Jordan, to recover millions of dollars that she claims belonged to her late husband Saddam Kamel; suit focuses attention of huge sums that are thought to be held by Pres Hussein's family, particularly Maj Gen Hussein Kamel and his brother Saddam, sons-in-law who defected to Jordan in 1995 and were killed when they returned to Iraq in Feb 1996 (M)

1998-05-29.title: NUCLEAR ANXIETY
1998-05-29.abstract: AFTERMATH -- Experts say they fear an India-Pakistan war and a new arms race. News analysis, page A9.

1998-05-29.title: China Voices 'Regret,' but Still Faults India
1998-05-29.abstract: China, Pakistan's oldest and most powerful Asian ally, expresses 'deep regret' over Pakistan's decision to conduct nuclear tests, but officials reiterate opinion that India touched off crisis with its earlier tests (S)

1998-05-29.title: PAKISTAN, ANSWERING INDIA, CARRIES OUT NUCLEAR TESTS; CLINTON'S APPEAL REJECTED
1998-05-29.abstract: Pakistan carries out underground nuclear tests in response to India's recent detonations; Prime Min Nawaz Sharif declares his country a 'nuclear power,' fulfilling secret plan made by predecessor thirty years ago; photos; Government says it is fitting nuclear warheads on missile that could strike most of north and central India; Sharif's announcement that Pakistan has 'evened the score with India' sets off pandemonium in Indian Parliament and raises specter of retaliation; explosions are carried out at test range in Chagai Hills, remote southwest desert near Afghan and Iran borders; Pakistanis celebrate in streets; Government declares state of emergency; analysts believe two weapons were tested, not five announced; Pres Clinton, who made calls to Sharif appealing for restraint, quickly announces US sanctions; says he cannot believe new century will start with Indian subcontinent repeating worst mistakes of 20th century; Sharif tells Clinton he is bowing to overwhelming domestic pressure from military chiefs, Muslim clerics and opposition leaders like Benazir Bhutto, who vowed to take to streets unless Sharif acted; her late father, Zulfikar Ali Bhutto, committed to covert nuclear program in 1970's to make 'Islamic bomb' (L)

1998-05-29.title: Yeltsin to Ask Western Help for Ruble as Markets Steady
1998-05-29.abstract: Pres Boris Yeltsin will ask Western leaders to help defend Russian rubble, even as Russian financial markets steady slightly after record plunge; Yeltsin will ask Pres Clinton and German Chancellor Helmut Kohl to support Russia as it plans new round of spending cuts and more vigorous tax collecting; tells foreign investors there will be no collapse, that central bank and finance ministry have enough reserve funds to stay afloat; IMF envoy John Odling-Smee arrives to confirm $700-million installment of $10 billion loan; Anatoly B Chubais will talk with officials in Washington; deputy minister Oleg Vyugin says Russia needs psychological boost of more foreign credits, even if they are not needed; photo; Russian RTS stock index gains 6.2 percent, winning back most of previous day's losses; key test will be auction of Government-backed bonds at interest rate of 150 percent; Yeltsin warns that 'heads will roll' unless tax collectors improve dismal collection rates (M)

1998-05-29.title: Indonesian Students Resume Demonstrations in Capital
1998-05-29.abstract: Indonesia students resume demonstrations in Jakarta, gathering in holiday mood outside Parliament to reject new Government timetable for elections next year; demands for immediate voting are repeated by Muslim leader Amien Rais; student movement itself is curiously leaderless, but observer notes that 'republic of fear' has fallen in country; photo (S)

1998-05-30.title: Clinton Meets Delegation From Kosovo Seeking Talks
1998-05-30.abstract: Pres Clinton meets with ethnic Albanian leaders from Serbian province of Kosovo who favor negotiated solution to their conflict with Serbian Government; White House meeting is intended to display US interest and involvement in Kosovo, as well as support for Ibrahim Rugova, president of self-declared Republic of Kosovo (M)

1998-05-30.title: SUHARTO FORTUNE DRAWING NEW FIRE
1998-05-30.abstract: Attacks intensify in Indonesia on Suharto family as two of former Pres Suharto's relatives are forced out of their positions and new Government announces several measures aimed at curbing economic favoritism that allowed family's fortunes to flourish; officials demand return of up to $40 billion that Suharto and his six children siphoned from country's economy; Suharto's son Bambang Trihatmodjo resigns as president-director of Bimantara Citra conglomerate, one of country's largest companies; both he and Suharto's son-in-law, Indra Rukmana, quit company's board of directors (M)

1998-05-30.title: Wake-Up Call: Truisms Of U.S. Policy Totter
1998-05-30.abstract: R W Apple Jr analysis contends that several of hopes and assumptions of post-cold-war era have been shaken by momentous events in Asia; maintains that collapse of Pres Suharto's rule in Indonesia and detonation of nuclear devices by India and Pakistan have forced rethinking in Washington and other Western capitals of ideas that had come to be policy truisms; holds that widely acknowledged ascendancy of US commercial and economic interests over strategic and other goals, which led to subordination of human-rights considerations in region, is likely to be questioned much more sharply then previously (M)

1998-05-30.title: LEADERS IN INDIA AND IN PAKISTAN TONE DOWN CRISIS
1998-05-30.abstract: India and Pakistan begin talking of new arrangements that would stabilize their rivalry and mini imize risk of war, day after Pakistan matches India by setting off its own underground nuclear tests; neither country seems ready to consider agreement not to deploy nuclear weapons, especially missiles, and there are many signs that situation remains volatile; US officials express skepticism that calmer reality has set in (M)

1998-05-30.title: Gucheng Journal; In China's Countryside, It Is Time to Grow Rich
1998-05-30.abstract: Farmers in Gucheng and other villages of China's heartland are thriving as nation's urban workers struggle with layoffs and factory shutdowns; branching out from farming into business, they are creating miniature boom towns; late Chinese leader Deng Xiaoping gave Gucheng new life 18 years ago when he dissolved inefficient communal farms and doled out parcels of land for families to farm; over next decade farmers modernized farming so much that raising crops was no longer full-time job; map; photo (

1998-05-31.title: TV Journalists Find Yeltsin Drawn to Big Brother Role
1998-05-31.abstract: Russian Pres Boris N Yeltsin has called in directors of three national television channels to complain about negative coverage this week of crash on financial markets, floods in Siberia and miners' protest that blocked country's main railroad; Yeltsin said he was not trying to dictate how Russia's media should cover events, but urged TV channels to carry out state policy on television; Yeltin's comments come at sensitive time; this month Government has started putting together state company that will coordinate operations of state-owned television and radio stations, regional as well as national, and also manage sites that transmit private channels' broadcasts (M)

1998-05-31.title: PAKISTAN SETS OFF ATOM TEST AGAIN, BUT URGES 'PEACE'
1998-05-31.abstract: Pakistan explodes another nuclear test device, spurning worldwide criticism and calls for restraint after its nuclear tests two days ago; Shamshad Ahmad Khan, senior official in Foreign Ministry, indicates nuclear weapons can now be swiftly made ready for use; says test concludes the series; holds Pakistan is ready to 'talk peace' with India and that it is not Pakistan's purpose to enter into arms race; Pakistani tests come two weeks after India exploded what it said were five nuclear devices; US, which has imposed economic sanctions on Pakistan and India, condemns latest Pakistani test; test is conducted in remote Baluchistan region of desert and mountains near Iranian border where previous tests by Pakistan were conducted; photos (M)

1998-05-31.title: New Delhi's Leaders Say They Don't Need to Match Tests in 'Competition'
1998-05-31.abstract: India delays formal response to Pakistan's latest nuclear test, but Pramod Mahajan, leading Hindu nationalist politician and political adviser to Prime Min Atal Bihari Vajpayee, says India does not regard nuclear testing as 'competition' requiring Indian Government to match each move by Pakistan; Pres K R Narayanan, who is on state visit to Nepal, is quoted by Indian television stations as saying India will not conduct any more tests of its own (M)

1998-05-31.title: REELING INDONESIA GETS FISCAL BREAK
1998-05-31.abstract: International Monetary Fund official Hubert Neiss says economic situation in Indonesia is far worse that earlier estimated and that fund will relax its deadlines for imposition of austerity measures; Neiss says Indonesia's economy could shrink by at least 10 percent this year--double IMF's estimate earlier this year--as soaring inflation and unemployment take its toll; stresses that political stability will be crucial to economic recovery; newspaper Bisnis Indonesia reports that Government has canceled four port service contracts owned by Hutomo Mandala Putra, son of former Pres Suharto; newspaper Jakarta Post reports that police are reviewing contracts with company owned by Suharto's eldest daughter, Siti Hardiyanti Rukmana, for processing driver's licenses; corrupt practices and nepotism of Suharto Government have been major target of reforms demanded by IMF; photo of Neiss (M)

1998-05-31.title: With Hardly a Stir, Party Leaves Japan Coalition
1998-05-31.abstract: Japan's four-year-old governing coalition fractures when Social Democratic Party formally announces it will quit and strike out on its own; Liberal Democratic Party, which has dominated Japanese politics since 1950's, will continue to govern Japan, with no need for new elections or Cabinet reshuffle (M)
